content.claim: In England, Education, Health and Care Plan (EHCP) awards rose 71% between 2018 and 2024 (from 180,000 to 252,000), with the Institute for Fiscal Studies identifying finance-design mechanisms—ring-fenced funding blocks, frozen cash-terms thresholds, and EHCP-gated top-up funding—as plausible drivers alongside expanded recognition of need
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Causal attribution remains contested; IFS frames multiple 'potential explanations' without isolating a single driver
System is itself undergoing active reform, suggesting instability and disputed assumptions about incentive effects
England's geographic variation in EHCP awards indicates mechanism does not operate uniformly across contexts
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
